title: beyeria calycina
intro: Beyeria calycina, also known as the yellow stinkwood, is a species of plant in the family Cneoraceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Beyeria calycina include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
